fact: January 12 is exactly 11 days into the new year, which means you've already broken about half your New Year's resolutions!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when squirrels start getting super cranky because they're running low on their buried acorn stash from fall.
The sun rises almost 2 minutes later today than it did on January 1st if you live in most of North America - winter is still stealing your morning light!
January 12 falls smack in the middle of Capricorn season, making it the most stubborn and goal-obsessed day of the month according to astrology fans.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, January 12 is peak summer - kids are probably at the beach while you're bundled up in winter coats!
This date sits exactly 3 weeks and 2 days after the winter solstice, meaning daylight is finally starting to creep back minute by minute in cold places.
Polar bears in the Arctic are at their fluffiest right now - their winter fur is so thick they can sleep comfortably on ice that would freeze your toes off in seconds!
January 12 is when many evergreen trees like pine and spruce are actually growing their tiniest bit, even in freezing weather - they're basically winter superheroes!
If you live somewhere snowy, the snow that falls around January 12 makes the most perfect snowball texture because the temperature is just right for packing.
In places like Canada and northern Europe, this is when the aurora borealis (northern lights) show up most dramatically because the nights are super long and dark.
January 12 is roughly when hibernating animals like bears and groundhogs are in their deepest sleep - they won't wake up for another 6-8 weeks!
The moon phase around January 12 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, it appears extra bright because winter air is clearer and less humid than summer air.
